title: Pairwise autoregressive hybrid scorer

queries: graph llm hybrid candidate scorer autoregressive pairwise multiclass ranking mrr
graph rag candidate interaction plus token sequence scoring autocomplete multiclass

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-enhanced generation quality depends on how retrieved evidence is consumed by the downstream decision interface.
papers.relevance: Supports combining complementary scoring views rather than forcing one compressed interface.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Graph evidence should be exposed through an interface matched to the actual answer-selection mechanism.
papers.relevance: Supports combining prompt-conditioned candidate interaction with explicit candidate-token scoring for autocomplete-style ranking.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Prompt-side task interface design remains a major gain source after encoder-side changes saturate.
papers.relevance: Supports another scoring-interface refinement after static and pairwise label-scorer variants remained globally insufficient.

evidence.why_now: EXP093 improved user-ltv over the stored strict baseline and slightly improved item-incoterms versus recent candidate-aware runs, but it still left salt below baseline and flipped user-churn negative. The codebase already contains a separate autoregressive multiclass candidate scorer, so the next justified step is to hybridize the new pairwise interaction path with that existing token-sequence scorer instead of inventing a new loss family.

hypothesis: The pairwise scorer captures candidate-conditioned interaction structure, but it still discards useful signal already present in the autoregressive candidate-token scoring path. A hybrid scorer that combines pairwise interaction scores with autoregressive candidate sequence scores should preserve more salt-side ranking discrimination than either path alone.
notes: First implementation target: for autocomplete multiclass tasks only, sum a candidate-conditioned pairwise interaction score with the existing autoregressive candidate-token score.
Do not mix new loss terms, sampling tweaks, or packaging changes into the first implementation.
Launched on 2026-05-07 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
First stable orchestrator status check shows all three windows up; user-churn and user-ltv completed DB load, while item-incoterms reached normal startup logging.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.
Outcome: user-ltv and item-incoterms both improved above their strict baselines, but user-churn regressed below baseline.
